repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Sibley College reports, theses and course materials

origination.label: Creator
origination.corpname: Sibley College of Mechanical Engineering and Mechanic Arts

did.abstract: Course materials, theses, and other materials pertaining to Sibley College.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Three volumes of course materials: two volumes for the Course in Practical Mechanics, circa 1889-1896 (part 1: Wood working; part 2: Metal working), and one volume for courses in the Machine Design department (1903-1904). The Machine Design volume includes course outlines and sample problem sets with illustrations, some on blueprint paper.

Four volumes of student Master's theses for the Sibley College School of Marine Construction, labeled "Examples of work in design and special problems," 1900-1903. Included are theses by Charles H. Delany, Paul Percy Bird, Horace Warren Deved, J.D. Clair Smith, G. Kohler, Sidney Graves Koon, William Alphonso Rowe, Leonard Griffin Shepard, and Richard J. Donovan. The theses are copiously illustrated with tables and graphs, most on blueprint paper.

Also, six volumes (two copies, three volumes each) of student and faculty reports on mechanical equipment (dated 1902), illustrated with photographs, cyanotypes, graphs, and tables. Vols. 1-2 have spine title "Mechanical laboratory equipment, students' reports." Vol. 1 focuses on a description of the history, operation, buildings, and equipment of Sibley College's Dept. of Experimental Engineering. Vol. 2 consists of reports on tests of mechanical equipment. Vol. 3 has spine title "Investigation and research. Effect of cold rolling, Prof. H. Diederichs," and consists of a report on the effect of cold rolling on the physical properties of steel, prefaced by a letter from H. (Herman) Diederichs to R.H. (Robert Henry) Thurston, Director of Sibley College.

Also a Sibley Mechanical and Experimental Laboratory notebook from Raymond Curtis Warner Class of 1907 including textual notes, photographs, charts, and test results. Includes notes from courses taught by G. Carpenter, R. L. Shipman, W. Van Winkle, and H. W. Riley. Many of the tests were also conducted with fellow students Maurice Du Pont Lee and Huntly Harris Gilbert.
Also, reports from various students, 1917-1918, for Professors Richardson, Gavett, Hook, Davis, and Vanderdoes.

odd.head: NOTES
odd.p: Bound in brown morocco with gilt lettering.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Sibley College reports, theses and course materials, #16-5-508. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Steel.
Marine engineering.

controlaccess.corpname: Sibley College of Mechanical Engineering and Mechanic Arts. School of Marine Construction
Sibley College of Mechanical Engineering and Mechanic Arts. Department of Experimental Engineering

did.unittitle: Course materials
Course in Practical Mechanics, pt. 1: Wood Working
Course in Practical Mechanics, pt. 2: Metal Working
Machine Design
Raymond Curtis Warner laboratory notebook

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains some later doodles relating to war and Hitler.

did.unittitle: School of Marine Construction: Examples of Work in Design and Special Problems (theses)
Charles H. Delany, "Balancing of Marine Engines"
Paul Percy Bird, "Design of Machinery of About 4500 Horse-Power"
Horace Warren Deved, "Design of a 10,000 I.H.P. Triple Expansion Marine Engine"
J.D. Clair Smith, "Design of Marine Engine and Boilers of 1800 I.H.P."
G. Kohler, "Design of Marine Machinery of About 1000 Indicated Horse-Power"
Sidney Graves Koon, "On the Longitudinal Strength of Steel Ships"
William Alphonso Rowe, "The Design of a Ship"
Leonard Griffin Shepard, "On the Design of a 1400 Ton Ocean Going Yacht"
Richard J. Donovan, "Strength of Ships"
Reports
Department of Experimental Engineering Mechanical Laboratory Equipment, Students' Reports, v. 1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Presentation of the laboratory and its equipment (texts and photographs).

did.unittitle: Department of Experimental Engineering Mechanical Laboratory Equipment, Students' Reports, v. 2
Investigation and Research. Effect of Cold Rolling, Prof. H. Diederichs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains the report of tests (text, drafts, stats and photographs).

did.unittitle: Department of Experimental Engineering, Mechanical Laboratory Equipment, Students' Reports, v. 1, copy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy 2. Presentation of the laboratory and its equipment (texts and photographs).

did.unittitle: Department of Experimental Engineering Mechanical Laboratory Equipment, Students' Reports, v. 2, copy
Investigation and Research. Effect of Cold Rolling, Prof. H. Diederichs, copy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy 2. Contains the report of tests (text, drafts, stats and photographs).

did.unittitle: Student reports
